expectedBehavior: Scores 6 complexity criteria: correctness, maintainability, performance, security, scalability, testability
Uses 7-criterion scoring with 1-5 scale per criterion
Produces weighted composite score (0-10) from all dimension ratings
Limits scope to MAX 30 files to prevent context exhaustion
Decomposes architecture into individually assessable components and boundaries
Identifies risk factors and complexity hotspots
Provides pros/cons analysis with supporting evidence from the codebase
Compares current approach against alternatives with scores

testCases.query: Assess the performance of our database queries
expectedBehavior: Scores complexity criteria focused on estimation and risk for database analysis
Spawns agent for deep criterion-level scoring and assessment of task complexity
Includes effort estimation for each recommended improvement action
Answers 'is this good?' with structured decomposition and risk assessment
Produces overall score with grade interpretation (A+ through F)
Generates actionable improvement suggestions with effort/impact
Stores assessment results in memory for trend tracking
